content:
❌ Rejected the contribution.
Comment: Binary output issue
I agree with 5DN1L about the binary output issue, thanks for your contribution and I hope you get a chance to update it.

❌ Rejected the contribution.
Comment: Binary output issue, and a few other issues raised 16 days ago still not yet fixed.

✅ Approved the contribution.
Comment: Fun quick clash, very clearly defined. I like clear and quick clashes.

It took me a few tries, but I got it!

print(random.choice(["FOUND", "LOST"]))
Should not be Reverse I think

Formatting
* Please study the formatting syntax on this page: https://www.codingame.com/playgrounds/40701/help-center/statement , and format your statement properly as described in the points below.

Binary output issue
* According to the contribution guidelines, binary outputs should be avoided for clashes. That's exactly the issue with your clash, as it allows only two possible answers (either "FOUND" or "LOST"). Please modify the output requirement to allow for more than two possible answers.

Title
* Please remove the word [Edit: redacted], as it feels like an advertisement in disguise. (I've also deleted your message that asks people to wishlist the game. Please don't promote your games here.)

Goal
* Please remove all newlines within each paragraph.

* Please move the formula for Manhattan distance from the Constraints section to the Goal section, since the formula is not a constraint.

Input
* Please format "Line 1", "Line 2" and "Next [[N]] lines" in <<bold>>.

* Please add "space-separated" after "Two" and "Three".

* Please remove the newline before "the position".

Output
* Please format "FOUND" and "LOST" as {{constants}}.

* Please remove the newline between "at" and "least".

Cases
* Each test and its validator should cover the same scenario (either both FOUND or both LOST) while using slightly different inputs (with similar magnitudes). Please revise the cases accordingly.

Thanks.

Thank you for your quick action. Below is the updated list of my comments:

Binary output (VERY IMPORTANT)
* Please fix the issue.

Input
* Line 1: Please add "Two space-separated integers" between "Line 1:" and "[[SX]]".

* Next N lines: Please add "Three space-separated integers" between "lines:" and "[[LX]]".

* The input description and the input format don't match:
  • The input description states that SX and SY are on the same line.
  • The cases show that SX and SY are provided on different lines.

Constraints
* Please change both upper bounds 1000 to 100.

* Please change 100 and 500 to 10.

Cases
* Corresponding changes are required after the output requirement is revised.

Thanks.

Please withdraw your approval as the clash doesn't follow the contribution guidelines: it suffers from the binary output issue.

Thanks.
